A simple AI-powered voice chatbot built entirely with Python and NumPy. The project, accepts both text and speech input, and responds using text-to-speech. It is a great beginner-friendly project for understanding natural language processing, speech recognition, and neural networks without using heavy machine learning frameworks.

Cognitive Engine is a modular AI agent framework for building intelligent, decision-driven systems. It combines rule-based logic, machine learning, and large language models (LLMs) to enable adaptable and extensible agents across multiple domains.

This repository presents the implementation of ADINT, a comprehensive six-phase machine learning framework designed to proactively detect threats in digital advertising content. The framework evaluates four distinct algorithms; Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, Bidirectional LSTM, and BERT

ChromaSight is an AI-powered assistive framework designed to provide instant visual accessibility for individuals with color vision deficiency. By bridging the gap between advanced machine learning and practical accessibility solutions, this project empowers users to achieve greater independence in their daily lives.

A hybrid machine learning framework for vehicle-specific accident risk prediction, combining historical and real-time environmental data to generate intelligent alerts for safer navigation in mountainous and high-risk road conditions.

The engine features a custom backtesting framework that achieved a 0.66 precision score during the 2022 bear market stress test, demonstrating high reliability in volatile environments.

This project presents a statistically validated machine learning framework to predict **health risk levels based on air pollution exposure** across Indian cities. Using CPCB air quality data, the model incorporates **lagged exposure features** to capture delayed health effects and improves prediction accuracy.

GPBoost is a machine learning framework that combines gradient boosting with Gaussian processes (GPs) or mixed effects models. The idea is to leverage the strengths of boosting (handling structured, tabular data, strong predictive power) together with Gaussian processes

Prahari is a lightweight, real-time DNS security framework designed to detect and mitigate DNS spoofing attacks, particularly Man-in-the-Middle (MITM) threats. The system integrates rule-based filtering, machine learning, and blockchain technology to provide a robust, scalable, and tamper-proof solution for modern network environments.

The project successfully transitioned from raw data ingestion (XLS/CSV) to a refined machine learning pipeline, achieving higher interpretability by mitigating multicollinearity and implementing advanced feature selection.

End-to-end machine learning project predicting referendum outcomes using real-world Swiss municipal data.   Evaluated model performance using RMSE within a Kaggle competition framework.   Demonstrates skills in data analysis, model selection, and reproducible ML workflows.

PhaseLearner is a modular machine-learning framework for extracting phase structure from many-body simulation data. Designed for DQMC and lattice models, it implements dimensionality reduction, latent-space analysis, and physics-informed deep learning models to locate critical parameters, identify symmetry breaking, and characterize emergent order.

Machine learning-based surrogate models offer a faster alternative for preliminary aerodynamic estimation. Framework to mitigate the privacy risks inherent in centralized cloud-based machine learning.
